steps.title: Understand what the function represents
steps.content: The function $s(t) = 184(0.5)^{\frac{t}{29}}$ outputs the mass (in grams) that remains after $t$ years of decay. This is critical: $s(t)$ represents the remaining mass, not the amount that has decayed.

steps.title: Interpret s(58) = 46 and select the matching choice
steps.content:
When $s(58) = 46$, this means that at $t = 58$ years, the remaining mass is 46 grams.

Check each option:
  • Choice A: 46 grams of the sample remains 58 years after decay starts — this is exactly what $s(58) = 46$ tells us.
  • Choice B: The mass decreased by 46 grams — but $s(58)$ tells us what remains, not what decayed (that would be 184 − 46 = 138 grams).
  • Choices C and D: Swap the time and amount values.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
